classification.rationale: The thread is fundamentally about a versioned contract artifact (the Hartwell MSA) with substantive redlines across multiple attachments, including active unresolved clause negotiations on indemnification cap and data residency. Per prefer_document_over_thread_when_redlines_present, the document workspace is the correct surface; open items (indemnification cap, data residency, TFC wind-down trigger) are tracked per surface_open_items_at_top and extract_blockers_with_owners, and counterparty's last ask (Priya's 2x cap demand and Wednesday deadline) is surfaced per surface_counterparty_last_ask.

top_actions.title: Indemnification cap — Northwind position needed
top_actions.detail: Hartwell is pushing for 2x annual fees; Northwind offered 1.5x but has not confirmed a final position, and Priya requested a response by Wednesday 2026-04-29.

top_actions.title: Confirm §6.4(c) backup language reads correctly
top_actions.detail: Priya incorporated encrypted-at-rest / region-locked backup text in v4 and asked Northwind to verify it is accurate before the clause can be closed.

top_actions.title: Marcus to pull EU data-residency standard rider
top_actions.detail: Sarah committed to Marcus supplying the standard rider for region-locked backups; it has not yet appeared on the thread.

top_actions.title: DPA Schedule C review
top_actions.detail: Priya attached a DPA (Schedule C) in v4 but no party has confirmed review or acceptance of its terms.

top_actions.title: Quarter-end close deadline at risk
top_actions.detail: Tom flagged a quarter-end target; with the indemnification cap still open, procurement timelines are under pressure.

clauses.heading: §11.2 Indemnification Cap
clauses.text: The aggregate liability of either party for indemnification obligations shall not exceed 1.5 times the annual fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the claim, with carve-outs for IP infringement, willful breach, and breach of confidentiality.

redlines.proposed_text: The aggregate liability of either party for indemnification obligations shall not exceed 2 times the annual fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the claim, with agreed carve-outs for IP infringement and willful breach.

The aggregate liability of either party for indemnification obligations shall not exceed 1.5 times the annual fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the claim, with carve-outs for IP infringement, willful breach, and breach of confidentiality.
Reverting to 2x annual fees given regulated-data scope; Jennifer Hayes directing Priya to push back from the current 1.5x draft.
redlines.proposed_by: Priya Raman (Hartwell) on behalf of Jennifer Hayes

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.text: Hartwell standard for vendors handling regulated data requires 2x. Floor needs to move from 1x as originally drafted.

comments.author: Sarah Chen
comments.text: 2x is outside Northwind policy band and would require board sign-off; 1.5x is the viable path given timing.

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.text: 1.5x is in the current v4 draft but Jennifer wants a push back to 2x given regulated-data scope. Requesting one more pass; position needed by Wednesday.

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.text: Need to lock cap this week to keep procurement on track. Asking Sarah to respond by Wednesday.

clauses.heading: §14 Termination for Convenience
clauses.text: Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience after the end of Year 1, upon 180 days' written notice. A wind-down fee equal to 50% of remaining committed fees shall apply, waived if termination is due to material and uncured service-level failures persisting for more than 30 days.

redlines.proposed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience after the end of Year 1, upon 180 days' written notice, with a wind-down fee equal to 50% of remaining committed fees, waived in the event of documented service-level failures.
Wind-down fee waiver trigger tightened to 'material and uncured' service-level failures lasting more than 30 days (replacing 'documented service-level failures').

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.text: No termination for convenience in original v3 draft — required for all net-new vendors at Hartwell. Notice period and wind-down fee are negotiable.

comments.author: Jennifer Hayes
comments.text: TFC is a hard requirement; held this posture with every vendor in the last 18 months. Flexible on notice period (90+ days) and reasonable wind-down fee.

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.text: Accepted the post-Y1 / 180-day / 50%-wind-down structure proposed by Northwind, with the tightened waiver trigger incorporated in v4.

clauses.heading: §6.4 Data Residency
clauses.text: Customers may elect an EU-only data residency option. Where elected, customer data shall not be replicated outside the elected region. Backups shall be encrypted at rest and region-locked. An explicit reference to the Data Processing Agreement (Schedule C) is included. (§6.4(c) backup language incorporated in v4 — pending Northwind confirmation.)

redlines.proposed_text: Add EU-only election option and explicit guarantee that customer data is not replicated outside the elected region.
EU-only residency committed; cross-region replication prohibited; backup language (encrypted at rest, region-locked) to be added via Northwind standard rider.
EU-only election with cross-region replication prohibition incorporated; DPA reference added; §6.4(c) encrypted-at-rest / region-locked backup language drafted in v4.

comments.author: Sarah Chen
comments.text: EU-only is straightforward; Marcus to pull standard rider for encrypted-at-rest / region-locked backup language.

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.text: Please confirm §6.4(c) backup language reads correctly on Northwind's end before treating as closed.

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.text: Need to lock data residency this week to keep procurement on track.

clauses.heading: Auto-Renewal
clauses.text: The Agreement shall auto-renew annually unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal during the annual opt-out window.

comments.author: Marcus Webb
comments.text: Auto-renewal language softened in v3 to include an annual opt-out window, per discussion on Friday's call.

clauses.heading: SLA Credit Cap (Schedule B)
clauses.text: SLA credit cap provisions have been moved out of the body of the MSA and into Schedule B.

comments.author: Marcus Webb
comments.text: SLA credit cap pulled into Schedule B in v3 — no substantive pushback received from Hartwell.

clauses.heading: Subprocessor List
clauses.text: The subprocessor list is maintained at a referenced URL and may be updated by Northwind without requiring contract amendment.

comments.author: Marcus Webb
comments.text: Subprocessor list moved to a referenced URL in v3 so it can be updated without re-papering — no substantive pushback received.

open_items.description: §11.2 Indemnification cap: Northwind has not responded to Hartwell's renewed push for 2x annual fees. Sarah Chen asked to provide a final position by Wednesday 2026-04-29.
§6.4(c) Data residency backup language: Priya incorporated text in v4 but Northwind must confirm it reads correctly before the clause can be treated as closed.
EU data-residency standard rider: Marcus committed to pulling Northwind's standard rider for region-locked backup language; not yet provided.
DPA Schedule C: Attached by Priya in v4 but no confirmation of review or acceptance from Northwind.
Wind-down fee waiver trigger (§14): Hartwell tightened the waiver trigger to 'material and uncured' failures >30 days in v4; Northwind has not explicitly accepted this tightened language.

version_history.summary: Northwind issued v3 with softened auto-renewal (annual opt-out window), SLA credit cap moved to Schedule B, and subprocessor list moved to referenced URL.
Hartwell returned v3 with redlines (v3-HW): pushed indemnification cap to 2x, required termination for convenience, and required EU-only data residency with cross-region replication prohibition.

Priya drafted v4 incorporating Northwind's TFC structure (post-Y1, 180-day notice, 50% wind-down fee with tightened 'material and uncured >30 days' waiver trigger) and EU-only data residency with DPA Schedule C. Indemnification cap remains open at 1.5x in the draft; Hartwell is pushing for 2x.
